title: ArcStatusEvent
description: An arc.status event as defined by the Agent Arc Status Protocol v0.2.

arc_id.description: Stable identifier for the arc. All events in one arc share this value. RFC 4122 UUIDs are RECOMMENDED.

phase.description: Lifecycle position of the arc at the moment of emission.

title.description: Short human-readable label for this event. Single line, no newlines.

body.description: Longer freeform body, optionally containing markdown.

step.description: 1-indexed step number within a sequenced arc.

total.description: Total number of steps in a sequenced arc.

eta_minutes.description: Emitter's current estimate of minutes remaining to done.

sent_at.description: UTC timestamp at which the event was generated, in RFC 3339 form. The grammar is a self-contained pattern (no `format` dependency) so the schema validates under any standard JSON Schema validator with no plugins.

arc_kind.description: Optional free-form label naming the kind of work. Reserved for consumer routing; the Protocol assigns it no semantics.

protocol_version.description: The Protocol version the emitter targets, e.g. 0.1.

$comment: JSON Schema 2020-12 cannot express two constraints this Protocol imposes; implementations validating only against this schema MUST apply them separately, and the reference implementation in `reference/node/` enforces both. (1) Cross-field: if both `step` and `total` are present, `step` MUST be ≤ `total`. (2) Calendar validity: the `sent_at` pattern bounds the day to 01–31 but cannot express per-month limits, so it admits impossible dates (e.g. 2026-02-30) that emitters MUST NOT produce and conformant validators MUST reject.
